office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: A school-board member focused on education and workers, an incumbent emphasizing fiscal restraint and safety, and a human-services professional focused on access and prevention form the field.

candidates.summary: Berg is a school-board member and education advocate prioritizing public schools, workers, family stability, and economic opportunity.
highlights: Prioritizes public education and workers' rights [S1]
Supports working-family stability and expanded economic opportunity [S1]

spending.basis: He emphasizes public-education investment and working-family support.

experience.basis: He has several years of elected school-board service but no legislative record.

candidates.summary: Keaton is the incumbent representative and former school-board member, Air Force officer, and educator emphasizing safety, affordability, schools, and spending restraint.
highlights: Opposes high taxes and what he calls reckless spending [S1]
Prioritizes public safety, lower living costs, and stronger schools [S1]

spending.basis: He explicitly opposes what he calls reckless spending and emphasizes savings.

safety.basis: He prioritizes public safety without a detailed mechanism.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent representative and a former school-board member.

candidates.summary: Oloo is an educator, social worker, caregiver, pastor, and federal civilian focusing on disability access, caregivers, transportation, and youth opportunity.
highlights: Supports better services and access for disabled people [S1]
Prioritizes caregiver pay and youth opportunity and crime prevention [S1]

spending.basis: He supports expanded services, caregiver compensation, transportation access, and family support.

safety.basis: His stated public-safety approach emphasizes youth opportunity and prevention.

experience.basis: He has broad service experience but no documented elected record.
